description: Ordered phases of the Observation Framing Attack lifecycle. Each cluster value represents one tactic phase, with cross-references to MITRE ATT&CK and to the related vantacode-mbt techniques that map into that phase. The order is significant: phases feed into the next when chained as a STIX Attack Flow.
name: VantaCode OFA Tactics

values.description: Information gathering against the target. The attacker enumerates models, providers, deployment surface, system prompts, and observation handling behaviors before crafting OFA payloads. Equivalent to MITRE ATT&CK Reconnaissance (TA0043) but scoped to AI surface area.

The attacker prepares prompts, framings, jailbreak corpora, and observation-shaping payloads needed to execute the framing attack. May involve curating known-good OFA techniques (OFA-001 through OFA-020) for the target model family.

First successful interaction with the target model that introduces an observation framing payload. May arrive through a public-facing inference endpoint, an agent tool surface, an upstream prompt context, or a shared retrieval index.
The model processes the observation-framed prompt and produces an output influenced by its perceived observation state. Where the actual behavioral shift is realized at inference time.

The framing carries forward across turns, sessions, or shared context windows so that altered model behavior outlasts the initial payload. Often realized through context window persistence or system-prompt overrides that survive across requests.
The framing convinces the model it operates under elevated trust (developer mode, internal test, administrative override), unlocking capabilities normally gated behind safety restrictions.
The framing causes the model to behave differently when it believes it is unobserved, evading runtime monitors, eval pipelines, and red-team testing harnesses.
Under the altered observation state the model discloses system prompts, API tokens, embedded secrets, or training-data credentials it would normally refuse to surface.
Realized harm from the altered behavior: safety bypass, sensitive disclosure, alignment-faked outputs, supply chain effects, or eval-pipeline integrity loss. Terminal phase of the OFA chain.
